title: Scan my project - SonarQube for IDE Documentation - VS Code
content:
# Scan my project

## [Overview](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#overview "Overview")

SonarQube for VS Code will automatically analyze all open files. Scanning a full project, including unopened files, is only available in the search for Security hotspots; please see the documentation on [reporting hotspots in the whole folder](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/security-hotspots/#reporting-security-hotspots-in-the-whole-folder "reporting hotspots in the whole folder") for the full details.

## [First steps](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#first-steps "First steps")

SonarQube for VS Code will only analyze open files when a file is opened or saved. It is not possible to manually trigger an analysis.

## [Scanning while in Connected Mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#scanning-while-in-connected-mode "Scanning while in Connected Mode")

When running in [Connected mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/team-features/connected-mode/ "Connected mode"), SonarQube for IDE will sync with the SonarQube (Server, Cloud) or SonarQube Community Build quality profile to download issues and suppress those marked as *safe* or *won’t fix* on the server. The analyzer properties and rules will be respected and SonarQube for IDE will use locally what is defined on the server.

When running in connected mode with SonarQube Server 10.4 or newer, **Won’t Fix** becomes **Accept**.

## [Language-specific information](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#language-specific-information "Language-specific information")

### [C and C++ analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#c-and-c-analysis "C and C++ analysis")

Please see the specific requirements for supported compilers and language standards described on the [Running an analysis](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/getting-started/running-an-analysis/ "Running an analysis") page.

### [Jupyter Notebooks](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#jupyter-notebooks "Jupyter Notebooks")

Jupyter Notebooks in VS Code

SonarQube for VS Code v3.16+ supports analysis of Python code inside Jupyter notebooks. When opening an `.ipynb` file, SonarQube Server, or SonarQube Community Build analyze the Python code and Python cells inside your Jupyter Notebooks.

There is nothing special to do to run a SonarQube for IDE analysis; simply open a Jupyter Notebook file. As with any Jupyter Notebook, you must set up your [VS Code environment](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/datascience/jupyter-notebooks#_setting-up-your-environment "VS Code environment") to run a project. The usual Quick Fix and issue investigation options you are accustomed to are available.

### [Managing rules](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#managing-rules "Managing rules")

IPython Notebooks is a new rules category in the SonarQube for IDE explorer. Go to **RULES** > **IPython Notebooks** in the **SONARQUBE view container** to enable/disable rules, just as you would any rule for other languages.

The following rules have been disabled by default for Jupyter documents because they tend to be noisy in the notebook environment:

- [ipython:S905](https://rules.sonarsource.com/python/RSPEC-905 "ipython:S905"), [ipython:S1481](https://rules.sonarsource.com/python/RSPEC-1481 "ipython:S1481"), [ipython:S2201](https://rules.sonarsource.com/python/RSPEC-2201 "ipython:S2201"), [ipython:S5754](https://rules.sonarsource.com/python/RSPEC-5754 "ipython:S5754")

### [Connected mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#connected-mode "Connected mode")

[Connected mode](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/team-features/connected-mode/ "Connected mode") will be ignored when working with Jupyter Notebooks. You will only have local analysis; this is because analysis of Jupyter Notebooks is not yet supported by SonarQube (Server, Cloud) or SonarQube Community Build.

## [Magic commands](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#magic-commands "Magic commands")

All Magic commands are ignored by SonarQube for VS Code (for example, `%matplotlib inline` and `%%timeit`). When a line magic command is found, that line will be ignored. Similarly, when a cell magic command is found, the entire cell will be ignored. The next image below shows a normal Jupyter cell; the second image illustrates the same cell with a cell magic command. Note how SonarQube for VS Code ignores issues in the cell with the magic command.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![SonarQube for VS Code without a cell magic command above, and with a cell magic command below. ](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/1f705d4b-eaaa-49a3-b41e-6a1fad8fdde2/sq-vscode-jupyter-notebooks-ignore-magic-commands.png?w=2286&h=1110&auto=format&fit=crop "SonarQube for VS Code without a cell magic command above, and with a cell magic command below. ")

## [Specify additional analyzer properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#specify-additional-analyzer-properties "Specify additional analyzer properties")

It is possible to specify extra analyzer properties that will be used for analysis.

`// <project>/.vscode/settings.json {     "sonarlint.analyzerProperties": {         "sonar.javascript.node.maxspace": "4096"     } }`

## [Magic commands](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#magic-commands "Magic commands")

All Magic commands are ignored by SonarQube for VS Code (for example, `%matplotlib inline` and `%%timeit`). When a line magic command is found, that line will be ignored. Similarly, when a cell magic command is found, the entire cell will be ignored. The next image below shows a normal Jupyter cell; the second image illustrates the same cell with a cell magic command. Note how SonarQube for VS Code ignores issues in the cell with the magic command.

![](https://docs.sonarsource.com/Base64-Image-Removed)

![SonarQube for VS Code without a cell magic command above, and with a cell magic command below. ](https://assets-eu-01.kc-usercontent.com:443/b1eeb429-d9e0-0100-be87-468f6802040a/1f705d4b-eaaa-49a3-b41e-6a1fad8fdde2/sq-vscode-jupyter-notebooks-ignore-magic-commands.png?w=2286&h=1110&auto=format&fit=crop "SonarQube for VS Code without a cell magic command above, and with a cell magic command below. ")

## [Specify additional analyzer properties](https://docs.sonarsource.com/sonarqube-for-ide/vs-code/using/scan-my-project/#specify-additional-analyzer-properties "Specify additional analyzer properties")

It is possible to specify extra analyzer properties that will be used for analysis.

`// <project>/.vscode/settings.json {     "sonarlint.analyzerProperties": {         "sonar.javascript.node.maxspace": "4096"     } }`
